checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is block RLP size validated before any processing occurs (EIP-7934)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the entry point where blocks are received from P2P network. 2. Verify that len(rlp.encode(block)) > MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE check occurs BEFORE any RLP decoding or block header parsing. 3. Craft a block payload exceeding MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE and verify rejection at the network layer. 4. Confirm that oversized blocks do not trigger resource-intensive operations before rejection.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-1, Trust Boundary: tb-010. EIP-7934 specific defense. A P2P peer can send arbitrarily large block payloads.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are blocks with non-empty ommers rejected in post-merge Ethereum?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create a block with block.ommers set to a non-empty tuple. 2. Submit block via P2P network. 3. Verify that state_transition rejects the block with an appropriate error. 4. Verify that validate_header also enforces this constraint (defense-in-depth).
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-2, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Post-merge invariant. Non-empty ommers could trigger pre-merge code paths.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Do all 8 computed roots/hashes match header values after block execution?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. For each of the 8 fields (gas_used, transactions_root, state_root, receipt_root, bloom, withdrawals_root, blob_gas_used, requests_hash), create a block with a mismatched computed vs header value. 2. Verify that state_transition rejects each case. 3. Check that comparison is performed with constant-time equality for cryptographic hashes. 4. Verify no early-exit on first mismatch allows partial state to be committed.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-3, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-005. Bypass allows invalid state transitions leading to consensus failure.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is transaction sender identity correctly derived from ECDSA signature?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify recover_sender correctly implements ECDSA recovery from (v, r, s) signature. 2. Test with malleable signatures (high-s values) and verify rejection per EIP-2. 3. Test edge cases: s=0, r=0, v outside valid range, points on curve boundaries. 4. Attempt signature forgery using known attack vectors and verify detection.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-5, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Signature spoofing allows account takeover.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is RLP encoding deterministic and collision-free?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Test RLP encoding with various block structures and verify determinism. 2. Verify that for B1 != B2, rlp.encode(B1) != rlp.encode(B2). 3. Test with nested structures, empty fields, and maximum-size payloads. 4. Cross-check encoding output against reference implementations.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-6, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-005, tb-010. Non-deterministic encoding causes consensus splits.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is keccak256 collision-resistant and correctly implemented?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify keccak256 implementation produces correct test vectors. 2. Check for any implementation-specific optimizations that might introduce bugs. 3. Verify hash comparison uses constant-time equality. 4. Test with edge cases: empty input, single byte, maximum size inputs.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-7, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-005. Implementation bug in hash computation could enable block forgery.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is withdrawal amount scaled correctly by 10^9 (Gwei to Wei)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Trace the process_withdrawals function to verify the scaling factor of 10^9. 2. Test with withdrawal amounts at boundary values (0, max uint64 Gwei). 3. Verify no overflow occurs during multiplication. 4. Confirm balance increase exactly equals withdrawal.amount * 10^9 Wei.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-8, Trust Boundaries: tb-003, tb-007. Incorrect scaling affects total ETH supply.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does withdrawal processing only increase balances, never decrease?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review process_withdrawals implementation for any subtraction operations. 2. Test for integer overflow that could cause wraparound to lower values. 3. Verify balance_after(a) >= balance_before(a) for all accounts a. 4. Test with maximum balance values approaching overflow boundaries.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-9, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Balance decrease during withdrawal is fund loss.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Do system contracts contain code before checked system transactions execute?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify process_checked_system_transaction checks len(system_contract_code) > 0. 2. Test at fork boundary to ensure system contracts are deployed. 3. Verify InvalidBlock is raised when contract has no code. 4. Note: unchecked variants (SG-010) do not have this check.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-10, Trust Boundary: tb-004. Missing system contract code causes block processing failure.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are checked system transaction errors propagated as InvalidBlock?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify process_checked_system_transaction raises InvalidBlock if system_tx_output.error is not None. 2. Identify ways to cause system contract reversion (WITHDRAWAL_REQUEST_ADDRESS, CONSOLIDATION_REQUEST_ADDRESS). 3. Verify that error check cannot be bypassed. 4. Test what happens if system contract can be made to revert on every block.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-11, Trust Boundaries: tb-004, tb-011. If system contract reverts on every block, chain halts.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are unchecked system transactions only used for beacon roots and history storage?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify that process_unchecked_system_transaction is only called for BEACON_ROOTS_ADDRESS and HISTORY_STORAGE_ADDRESS. 2. Confirm that failure of these contracts is non-critical (affects ring buffer data only). 3. Verify a compromised CL cannot corrupt critical state via unchecked system transactions.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-12, Trust Boundary: tb-004. Unchecked variants allow silent failures for non-critical operations.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Do all blob versioned hashes have 0x01 version prefix?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit blob transaction with versioned hashes having non-0x01 prefix (e.g., 0x00, 0x02). 2. Verify rejection at transaction validation stage. 3. Verify EL only checks version byte; KZG proof verification is by CL. 4. Test with hashes that have correct prefix but invalid KZG commitments.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-15, Trust Boundary: tb-008. Non-KZG versioned hash breaks data availability guarantees.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is gas refund capped at gas_used // 5 (EIP-3529)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Craft transaction with operations generating large refund counters (e.g., SSTORE clearing). 2. Verify tx_gas_refund = min(gas_used // 5, refund_counter). 3. Test boundary cases where refund_counter exceeds gas_used // 5. 4. Verify refund never exceeds 20% of gas consumed.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-16, Trust Boundary: tb-006. Excess refunds enable spam at reduced cost.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is EIP-7623 calldata floor gas cost enforced after refund?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Craft transaction with large calldata and refund-generating operations. 2. Verify tx_gas_used = max(gas_used - refund, calldata_floor_gas_cost). 3. Test that final gas used is at least the calldata floor. 4. Test boundary cases where refunds would push below floor.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-17, Trust Boundary: tb-006. Bypassing floor allows cheap data availability exploitation.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are post-merge header fields (difficulty, nonce, ommers_hash) correctly zeroed?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create block with non-zero difficulty, non-zero nonce, or non-EMPTY_OMMER_HASH ommers_hash. 2. Verify validate_header rejects each case. 3. Verify defense-in-depth: state_transition also enforces these constraints. 4. Check for client-specific divergence on pre-merge code paths.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-18, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Non-zero post-merge fields could trigger pre-merge code paths.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are sender accounts with code validated for EIP-7702 delegation designations?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction from account with code that is NOT a valid delegation designation. 2. Verify check_transaction rejects the transaction. 3. Test EIP-7702 authorization list validation for bypass vectors. 4. Verify contracts cannot initiate unauthorized transactions.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-19, Trust Boundary: tb-009. Bypass allows unauthorized code execution over victim accounts.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is transaction gas checked against remaining block gas?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction with tx.gas > (block_gas_limit - block_gas_used). 2. Verify check_transaction rejects the transaction. 3. Test at boundary: tx.gas exactly equals remaining gas. 4. Test for integer overflow in gas calculation.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-21, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Bypass causes block gas limit overrun and consensus failure.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is transaction blob gas checked against remaining block blob gas?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit blob transaction with calculate_total_blob_gas(tx) > blob_gas_available. 2. Verify check_transaction rejects the transaction. 3. Test for overflow in total_blob_gas calculation. 4. Test at exact boundary values.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-22, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-008. Bypass allows excess blob data breaking data availability assumptions.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is max_fee_per_gas >= max_priority_fee_per_gas enforced for EIP-1559 transactions?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit EIP-1559 transaction with max_priority_fee_per_gas > max_fee_per_gas. 2. Verify check_transaction rejects the transaction. 3. Test for arithmetic underflow in effective gas price calculation. 4. Test boundary case: priority fee exactly equals max fee.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-23, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Inconsistent fee structure could cause underflow.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is max_fee_per_gas >= base_fee_per_gas enforced?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction with max_fee_per_gas < current base_fee_per_gas. 2. Verify check_transaction rejects the transaction. 3. Verify rejection occurs before execution, not after. 4. Test at exact boundary: max fee equals base fee.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-24, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Acceptance allows under-priced execution.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Do blob transactions have between 1 and BLOB_COUNT_LIMIT blobs?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit blob transaction with 0 blobs (empty blob_versioned_hashes). 2. Submit blob transaction with > MAX_BLOB_COUNT blobs. 3. Verify both cases are rejected by check_transaction. 4. Test at exact boundaries: 1 blob and MAX_BLOB_COUNT blobs.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-25, Trust Boundary: tb-008. Zero blobs wastes capacity; excess breaks data availability.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are blob and SetCode transactions prevented from creating contracts?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit blob transaction with tx.to = None (contract creation). 2. Submit SetCode transaction with tx.to = None. 3. Verify check_transaction rejects both cases. 4. Test for bypasses using special address values.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-26, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-008, tb-009. Allowing contract creation violates EIP semantics.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Do SetCode transactions require non-empty authorization lists?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit SetCode transaction with empty authorization list. 2. Verify check_transaction rejects the transaction. 3. Test for bypasses with malformed authorization entries. 4. Verify this indicates broader EIP-7702 validation coverage.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-27, Trust Boundary: tb-009. Empty list is semantically invalid.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does sender nonce match transaction nonce exactly?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction with nonce < sender_account.nonce. 2. Submit transaction with nonce > sender_account.nonce. 3. Verify both are rejected by check_transaction. 4. Test for nonce verification bypass vectors enabling replay attacks.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-28, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-006. Nonce bypass enables replay or ordering attacks.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is sender balance sufficient for maximum possible transaction cost?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify balance check: sender_balance >= (tx.gas * max_fee_per_gas) + tx.value + (blob_gas * max_fee_per_blob_gas). 2. Test with extreme gas parameters near uint256 boundary to trigger overflow. 3. Test with sender balance exactly at required amount. 4. Verify no integer overflow in cost calculation.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-29, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-006. Bypass creates ETH from nothing -- critical fund loss.
